programme.desc: The drivers are ready for what is forecast to be a cold and busy winter, but the tragic passing of legendary driver Darrell Ward leaves the drivers wondering who can take his place.

programme.title: Ice Road Truckers
programme.desc: Lisa Kelly finds herself in a real jam after jackknifing her truck deep in the back-country; Alex Debogorski gets more than he bargained for when he falls through a patch of thin ice; Todd Dewey comes to the aid of a stranded trucker.

programme.title: Ice Road Truckers
programme.desc: A sudden warm-up threatens to derail the ice road season; Art Burke faces scary and slick roads; Lisa Kelly is forced to ask her former boss for financial help; Alex Debogorski encounters a mystery man walking alone on an isolated winter road.

programme.title: Ice Road Truckers
programme.desc: A blast of arctic air reopens the ice roads; Steph Custance falls victim to severe icing and winds up in a ditch; truckers set out to see if the newly reopened ice crossings are really ready for their big rigs.

programme.title: Neighborhood Wars
programme.desc: A woman films a shocking altercation between her neighbors in their apartment building parking lot, which ends in some truly dangerous driving; the story of a nurse who saved her neighbor's life.

programme.title: Neighborhood Wars
programme.desc: A woman is incensed by her neighbor's parking and takes revenge on her car; when a dog owner allows her pooch to poop on a neighbor's lawn and doesn't pick it up, the neighbor is watching and has a surprise in store.

programme.title: Christmas Wars
programme.desc: Workers installing a massive Christmas light display in New Zealand run into some extremely dangerous equipment issues; two co-workers at a retail store come to blows during a holiday party.

programme.title: Christmas Wars
programme.desc: A family in Canada cuts down a Christmas tree, with unintended consequences; in Washington, a woman pranks her grinchy husband with the assistance of their whole neighborhood; a Wisconsin woman discovers that some deals are too good to be true.

programme.title: Christmas Wars
programme.desc: A woman, fresh from a holiday party in Illinois, stands in the middle of the road and dares police to arrest her; a Christmas gift turns terrifying when it spontaneously bursts into flames inside a small Belgium apartment.

programme.title: Christmas Wars
programme.desc: A man dressed as Santa watches four women duke it out in a Massachusetts mall; a woman in Canada wrestles with a large hedge in what may be an attempt to get a free Christmas tree; a pregnant waitress in Pennsylvania gets an unexpected surprise.

programme.title: Christmas Wars
programme.desc: A parachuting Santa crash lands at a beach in Florida; an out of control giant balloon wreaks havoc at the Thanksgiving Day Parade in NYC; a Florida woman's Christmas tree is taken over by a mysterious furry visitor.

programme.title: Christmas Wars
programme.desc: In California, a paragliding Santa Claus has a head on collision with a power line; fists fly between two Black Friday shoppers at a mall in Kentucky; a Colorado driver chases down a delivery truck sprinkling the highway with last-minute gifts.

programme.title: Neighborhood Wars
programme.desc: In a scene straight out of a horror movie, an Oregon man is creeped out to discover a neighbor's hand repeatedly reaching under his fence; a student in New Hampshire learns a valuable life lesson when he gets into a scuffle with a neighbor.

programme.title: Neighborhood Wars
programme.desc: A commuter is given a neighborly greeting he'll never forget as a neighbor's car smashes into his own vehicle, narrowly avoiding a deadly disaster; in Florida, a family discovers a huge alligator taking a dip in their pool.

programme.title: THE NEW PINK PANTHER SHOW

programme.title: Danger Mouse

programme.title: Count Duckula: Mutinous Penguins

programme.title: THE NEW PINK PANTHER SHOW

programme.title: THE NEW PINK PANTHER SHOW

programme.title: Arthur: 1001 Dads/Prunella's Prediction
programme.desc: When Buster insists that he will miss the Father's Day picnic because he has other plans, Arthur is convinced that he's really sad and embarrassed about his parents’ divorce. And Prunella predicts a gift of flashpants, the hot new fashion.

programme.title: Arthur: Maria Speaks/Postcards from Binky
programme.desc: Maria has always been a silent fixture in Mr. Ratburn's third-grade class. But her time to speak has finally come. Can she fix her stutter? When Binky's grandparents miss his big soccer game, he decides to film a video reenactment.

programme.title: Arthur: Buenas Noches, Vicita/Prunella Packs It In
programme.desc: Vicita Molina has lost her favorite bedtime book – can Arthur help her re-create it? Prunella is already starting to think about college! Will she crack under the heavy load or will she learn to relax and do the things she actually enjoys?

programme.title: Arthur: Sue Ellen Adds It Up/Wish You Were Here
programme.desc: Math, schmath. That's what Sue Ellen thinks… until she runs into a problem her artistic vision can't solve. Sue Ellen can't wait to meet her pen pal in person.

programme.title: Arthur: Fernkenstein's Monster/D.W., Dancing Queen
programme.desc: Inspired by Mary Shelley's "Frankenstein," Fern tells a tale so scary that Arthur and the gang become afraid of her! Then, Binky decides to teach D.W. to dance, but she just doesn't seem to get the moves. Is D.W. a no-talent klutz?

programme.title: Arthur: Blabbermouth/All Grown Up
programme.desc: Buster is hurt when he learns his friends don't trust him to keep a secret. Then, the gang finds a mysterious fortune telling game at the library and gets a hint of what their futures could hold.

programme.title: Arthur: Francine's Cleats of Strength/Little Miss Meanie
programme.desc: When Francine needs money for new soccer cleats, Muffy hires her as a personal assistant. Lydia and Muffy join forces to defeat a mean beauty queen in the Little Miss Crocus" pageant.

programme.title: Arthur: Fernlets by Fern/Prunella and the Haunted Locker
programme.desc: Muffy talks Fern into writing poems for a new line of greeting cards which becomes the hit of Elwood City, but booming business causes Fern to burn out. Prunella is assigned a new school locker, which is rumored to be haunted!

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Barney can't stop snacking on pistachios! Barney helps BJ explore the deeper meaning of team sports and friendships.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Seeing - Beethoven's Hear!
programme.desc: An ordinary game of "hide and seek" takes a not-so-ordinary turn when Baby Bop claims that an elephant is hiding in the park! Barney and Riff are happy to play along, but BJ thinks that his sister may be "seeing things."

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: When she finds out that Daniel is going on a trip the next day, Baby Bop wants to go on a trip as well. Everyone decides to pretend to travel the same way Daniel will travel.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: That Makes Me Mad!
programme.desc: Barney helps his young friends find good ways to express and deal with angry emotions.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: At Home in the Park
programme.desc: The children work together to make a bird house. They are happy to learn that the bird will have lots of company; many animals make their homes in the park too!

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Baby Bop has trouble following the rules and taking turns while playing games. BJ hurts his toe while pretending to be Captain Pickles.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Look What I Can Do
programme.desc: Barney's friends are playing a fun game of "I Can Do" where they each share something they can do. When BJ suggests having an "I Can Do" show, Baby Bop becomes worried.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: The Blame Game - Airplanes
programme.desc: Marcos accidentally breaks an art show project that Melanie has made. Afraid that she will be angry, he doesn't say anything, but then feels guilty. Barney helps him see that honesty and taking responsibility for what we do is always best. together.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: My Baby Brother
programme.desc: Barney reminds Baby Bop and BJ that although babies start out little, they grow fast. Through pretend play, favorite songs and fun games, they learn about the many ways we grow every day.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: This is the tragic tale of Susan Smith, who shocked the nation in 1994 by fabricating a carjacking to cover up the drowning murders of her two young sons.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In January 2002, Ashley Pond and Miranda Gaddis disappear in Portland. Neighbor Ward Weaver, a suspect with a violent history, is later found guilty of their murders and sentenced to life in prison.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: On June 27, 2005, Dennis Rader, known as the BTK killer, pleads guilty to ten murders he committed in Wichita, Kansas, from 1974 to 1994. After a journalist's article reignites the case, Rader is arrested and sentenced to ten life terms without parole.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: On December 7, 1993, Colin Ferguson killed six and injured nineteen in the Long Island Railroad Massacre. Defending himself at trial, he bizarrely questioned his own victims and was sentenced to over 300 years in prison.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: On June 28, 2005, Joan Porco attends the trial of her son Christopher, accused of murdering his father and attacking her. Despite her initial identification and later refusal to testify, Christopher is found guilty based on circumstantial evidence.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In 1991, Ray and Faye Copeland, the oldest death row couple in the USA, are convicted of murdering five drifters. Police find the bodies after a tip-off. Faye's death sentence is commuted to life in 1999.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In July 2000, Sarah Payne is abducted and murdered. Roy Whiting, a convicted sex offender, is found guilty and sentenced to life in 2001. Sarah’s case leads to the implementation of “Sarah’s Law” in the UK in 2011.

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes
programme.desc: Newkirk poses as a traitor to send information via a propaganda radio broadcast.

programme.title: The Steve Wilkos Show: Bullied Out of School

programme.title: The Steve Wilkos Show: How Could You Cover for Him?
programme.desc: Gina believes that her daughter’s boyfriend Efrain is abusing her grandson. Her daughter wants to prove that Efrain is innocent, but her father Raul suspects she is covering for Efrain.

programme.title: The Steve Wilkos Show: I Got a 14 Year Old Pregnant
programme.desc: Megan is 14 and pregnant with 19 year old Michael’s baby, but he doesn’t see any problems with this situation. Laurie, Megan’s mother, turns to Steve to help get through to her!

programme.title: The Steve Wilkos Show: Who Did This?
programme.desc: Who’s responsible for toddler Noel's well-being? Everyone may deny the allegations, but someone knows what happened, and Steve is getting answers!

programme.title: The Steve Wilkos Show: Look What You Did to Your Baby
programme.desc: Kristy wants her baby back from her mother Donna! Donna claims she took the baby away because he was born with severe disabilities due to Kristy’s drug use during pregnancy. Who’s the liar?

programme.title: The Steve Wilkos Show: Your Kids Need Their Mom, But You Chose Him
programme.desc: Will Nadia and Woody's lie detector test results prove that they were negligent and deserved to lose custody of their children? Then, can Steve set the record straight about Josette's daughter?

programme.title: The Pearl of Death

programme.title: Pursuit to Algiers
programme.desc: Holmes is recruited to escort the heir to a European throne safely back to his homeland after his father's assassination.

programme.title: Sherlock - Dressed To Kill

programme.title: Hell's Kitchen USA

programme.title: Hell's Kitchen USA

programme.desc: On a limited budget in the historic L.A. neighborhood of West Adams, Allen and Ton still win three lockers and find a Maui Blue Fender Stratocaster and a 1930s Schwinn bike.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Nothing But Net

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Fire in the Hole!
programme.desc: 1930s "blasting machine" dynamite detonator box; 1890s treasure chest with secret compartments. Ton smelts jewelry to forge bricks of silver.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: The Wild West

programme.desc: Bloomberg TV is a live 24-hour global business and financial news channel covering the business world’s most important stories.

programme.title: The Takeout with Major Garrett
programme.desc: The Takeout is a new spin on the widely acclaimed podcast hosted by Major Garrett; a daily streaming show that harnesses the best of CBS News' political reporting and lives comfortably in deep discussions.

programme.title: The Daily Report
programme.desc: The Daily Report on CBS News features original interviews and insights on the most important, urgent and interesting stories of the day.

programme.title: 24/7 Primetime
programme.desc: Delivering the news you need to know.

programme.title: California Governor's Debate
programme.desc: Watch the free CBS News 24/7 live stream for breaking news & in-depth reporting.

programme.title: Arthur: Fernlets by Fern/Prunella and the Haunted Locker
programme.desc: Muffy talks Fern into writing poems for a new line of greeting cards which becomes the hit of Elwood City, but booming business causes Fern to burn out. Prunella is assigned a new school locker, which is rumored to be haunted!

programme.title: Arthur: Cast Away/The Great Sock Mystery
programme.desc: Arthur is thrilled when Dad invites him to go fly-fishing for the weekend. But a change in plans forces them to include D.W. Find out once and for all where socks disappear to when Baby Kate and Pal team up to solve the great sock mystery.

programme.title: Arthur: Binky's Music Madness/Brain Freeze
programme.desc: Everyone loves the Bang on a Can All-Stars, a funky experimental music group that's touring around Elwood City. Everyone that is, except Binky. A new ice cream shop has just opened up across the block. Will Brain's shop be able to survive?

programme.title: Arthur: The Cherry Tree/Matchmaker Matchbreaker
programme.desc: Muffy wants a Bouncy Castle at her party, but when her favorite cherry tree is cut down to make room for it, she starts to have regrets. Francine and Muffy scheme to set up their older siblings Chip and Catherine, but plans go awry.

programme.title: Arthur: Dear Adil/Bitzi's Break-Up
programme.desc: Arthur is worried that his letters to his new Turkish pen pal, Adil, will be boring. After all, how can Elwood City compare to Arthur's fantastical ideas about Turkey? Bitzi is breaking up with her boyfriend, and Buster is really sad.

programme.title: Arthur: The Agent of Change/D.W. Unties the Knot
programme.desc: The latest animated hit movie about a boy and his truck has left Muffy and Francine disappointed. Why doesn't there seem to be any cool female characters on screen? After watching "The Wedding Channel," D.W. wants to get married!

programme.title: Arthur: D.W. Tale Spins/Prunella Gets It Twice
programme.desc: What happens when you combine Greek Mythology with D.W.'s imagination? Then, getting two Polly Locket dolls almost ruins Prunella's birthday party. That, and Francine's bad mood--why, she wouldn't even sing the Happy Birthday song!

programme.title: Arthur: The Blackout/Mei Lin Takes a Stand
programme.desc: It's a hot day in Elwood City, and a total blackout leaves its residents without electricity! Will Arthur and DW survive without AC and TV? Binky's baby sister Mei-Lin isn't keen on learning to walk and would rather stick to crawling.

programme.title: Arthur: Arthur Babysits/Arthur's Cousin Catastrophe
programme.desc: You think running a marathon is hard? Try babysitting for the Terrible Tibble Twins. Next, Arthur's family is hosting the once-a-year-is-more-than-enough family reunion. But a major thunderstorm turns this into a reunion to remember.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: My Baby Brother
programme.desc: Barney reminds Baby Bop and BJ that although babies start out little, they grow fast. Through pretend play, favorite songs and fun games, they learn about the many ways we grow every day.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Movement - Separation
programme.desc: When some children in the park invite Barney to join them for a race, BJ and Baby Bop decide he'll need their help. They show him several ways to get ready for the big race by warming up and stretching.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Three kids try to build a pretend farm with disastrous results because they won't work together. Barney and his friends try to cheer up Olivia.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: As he watches birds fly overhead, Riff begins to daydream of flying himself. After seeing all the Moms playing with their kids in the Park, Baby Bop wonders what makes somebody a Mommy.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Venice, Anyone?
programme.desc: Emma can't play with her friends because she has to complete a school project about her family. Before you know it, Barney brings Emma on a trip to Venice to learn more about her great granddad.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: BJ and Riff find a pirate map, but in order to find the treasure, they have to be able to count. It’s time for a music concert in the park.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Baby Bop can't find anybody to play with her. When the others see her feeling sad, they decide to play whatever she wants to play. The dinos and kids are planning a camp-out at the park.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: A creepy spider inspires Barney to tell the story of “The Reluctant Dragon.”

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): What's Michelangelo Good For?
programme.desc: Dr. Lesseau is stealing animals from the city zoo to use for experiments.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): The Dimension X Story
programme.desc: April and Vernon of Channel Six News each need a big story, and Shredder's challenge to the Turtles of a fight to the finish sounds like just the ticket.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Donatello's Degree
programme.desc: To pick up a correspondence course degree addressed to "Miss Donna Tello," Donatello talks Irma into playing genius-for-a-day.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): The Big Cufflink Caper
programme.desc: When Shredder hires gonzo gangster Big Louie to find the explosive Cufflinks of Cathay, the Turtles go undercover with the mob to get to the bottom of the plot. Gunfights galore lead to a final shootout at the taffy factory at high midnight.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Leonardo Versus Tempestra
programme.desc: Tempestra's Revenge, the toughest game in video arcade, has got Leonardo hooked. Just as he's finally about to beat the machine, lightening strikes, and an electrical overload brings the wicked Tempestra to life.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Splinter Vanishes!
programme.desc: First Master Splinter vanishes, leaving behind instructions that the Ninja Turtles must now disband and go their separate ways.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Raphael Drives Em' Wild
programme.desc: Raphael's brain inadvertently gets switched with that of Oscar the cab driver.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Beyond the Donatello Nebula
programme.desc: Donatello's efforts to contact life in the Turtle Nebula succeed only too well: he accidently transports a space-traveling lizard named Algy down to Earth.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): The Big Bug Blunder
programme.desc: After Bebop and Rocksteady ruin an experimental batch of mutagen, Krang discovers that this new goo grows giant-sized insects. Shredder immediately sends some of the humongous bugs to Earth.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): The Foot Soldiers are Revolting
programme.desc: Desperate for decent henchmen, Shredder decides to make some minions he can rely on, and uses Krang's brain wave enhancer on a robotic foot soldier.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy stands in front of the pantry, desperate. The stock of chocolate spread is completely depleted. He realises that the Lemmings are making some themselves, following a complicated recipe.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Inspired by a magazine with a jewellery ad showing a man offering a diamond to a woman, Grizzy imagines himself offering the ranger's engagement ring to his beloved she-bear.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: When he accidentally sticks a computer microprocessor to his temple, Grizzy is transformed into an exceptionally intelligent being capable of predicting events to come far in advance.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: The Lemmings prepare a dream afternoon snack with the last jar of chocolate spread in the cupboard. Tough luck, Grizzy is coming. Caught off guard, the Lemmings hide the jar in a teddy bear-pyjama case to slip away without arousing suspicion.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy gleefully discovers that he can print images taken with the ranger's camera as it's connected to a state-of-the-art printer. He decides to frame himself, but when the time comes to print his portrait, there's no more paper in the printer.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: A van pulls up to the ranger's cabin to deliver a full crate of jars of chocolate spread. While Grizzy and the Lemmings fight over a wrecking bar to open it, someone steals the crate.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: As usual, Grizzly picks the door of the Ranger's log cabin to come in and make himself comfortable, but this time it's impossible to enter. The Lemmings have requisitioned the cabin for a party and barricaded themselves inside.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: The Lemmings have got their hands on some white beans which have very strange properties, when mixed with soda anyone who eats them turns into a bouncing ball. Grizzy realises that if he wants to have a nap, he must get rid of the magic beans.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzly wants to get rid of the old piano in the garage at any cost, for the Lemmings love using it to make a dissonant racket. But when he notices a she-bear he's in love with likes the melody of Fur Elise, he decides to learn the piece.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: The Lemmings inherit a crate that fell off a Canadian army convoy transporting ultra-secret material. The case inside the crate emits a sort of laser beam that divides objects into multiple miniaturised versions of themselves.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Near the supermarket, a machine hands out surprise eggs in exchange for a coin. Amongst the lots to be won, there is a super firework rocket the Lemmings dream of.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: At the Ranger's home, Grizzy finds a high-tech gadget that enables you to learn anything within seconds thanks to electrodes hooked to the brain, meaning that he can become the perfect bear.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: An old metal box found by chance in a recess of a cliff brings back childhood memories for Grizzy and the Lemmings.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy struggles to watch a documentary on salmon fish, nice and comfy on the sofa, as the Lemmings play chemistry novices with a fun experiment box.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy's TV remote control is stolen by a partridge who collects high-tech objects in his nest perched high up in a tree. This is a case of absolute urgency.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy has got it into his head to offer a butterfly to the bear he's in love with. But the Lemmings fight with him over the rare specimens he's spotted.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: The Lemmings have discovered an addictive new game based on the astonishing bouncing properties of the ranger's new running shoes.

programme.title: Pokemon: Advanced Battle
programme.desc: Ash can't resist challenging a Trainer who claims to have the world's most powerful Pokémon, but there's something odd about this battle…

programme.title: Pokemon: Advanced Battle
programme.desc: After meeting a group of Mightyena, Max befriends a Poochyena who hasn't evolved yet and decides to train it himself!

programme.title: Pokemon: Advanced Battle
programme.desc: Our heroes meet three sisters who grow berries with the help of a nearby group of Lotad!

programme.title: Pokemon: Advanced Battle
programme.desc: Ash and friends discover a whole new kind of Pokémon competition, and it seems May has found her calling!

programme.title: Pokemon: Advanced Battle
programme.desc: As May sets out to catch a Wurmple for her first Pokémon Contest, Ash receives a Double Battle challenge!

programme.title: Pokemon: Advanced Battle
programme.desc: Our heroes check out the Pokémon Trainer's School in Rustboro City, where Max has a run-in with a classmate!

programme.title: Pokemon: Advanced Battle
programme.desc: Armed with Pikachu's new Iron Tail attack, Ash challenges Gym Leader Roxanne!

programme.title: Pokemon: Advanced Battle
programme.desc: As our heroes tour the massive Devon Corporation, a mysterious intruder tries to steal an important piece of equipment!

programme.title: Pokemon: Advanced Battle
programme.desc: Mr. Briney the sailor offers to take our heroes to Dewford Island for Ash's next Gym battle, but then changes his mind. What's going on?

programme.title: Pokemon: Advanced Battle
programme.desc: When a school of Sharpedo attacks, Ash and friends are stranded on an uncharted island!

programme.title: Pokemon: Advanced Battle
programme.desc: Ash challenges the Dewford Gym, but Gym Leader Brawly seems more interested in surfing!

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch some children playing with torches. In Teletubbyland, Po chases Tinky Winky, and Po catches him.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: A voice trumpet rises & makes lovely roaring sounds, Dipsy & Laa-Laa laugh and run away. Some children pretend to be different animals and guess what they are. Tinky Winky hears a big roar and runs away.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Po pops up between Dipsy and Tinky Winky. They join Laa-Laa to watch two girls go for a tram ride around San Francisco. Laa-Laa and Po go for a walk. Laa-Laa goes the quick way and Po goes the long way.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies pop up to say hello before they gather round to watch some children learn about the number nine. Po and Tinky Winky count nine flowers that appear in Teletubbyland and then run off to fin Dipsy and Laa-Laa and dance the Bump dance.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies appear one at a time to say ‘Eh-oh’. They watch some children playing with bubbles. It’s time for Tubby Custard. The custard machine makes a funny noise, and a tubby custard bubble comes out followed by more.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch Daniel and Stephanie make big round pancakes. They are having a round and round day. They run round and round in Teletubbyland, but they stop to see the dancing bear in the Carousel, then there's time for one more dance.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch a Spanish girl play her violin for her friends. In Teletubbyland the Teletubbies watch Po dance.

programme.title: Teletubbies

programme.desc: A mechanical problem at the back of an Air Canada DC-9 turns into an emergency 10. The pilot has to attempt an emergency landing. The plane goes up in flames 90 seconds after it hits the runway.

programme.title: Mayday Air Disaster
programme.desc: Korean Air Flight 801 crashes five km short of its destination. More than 200 people on board are killed. The crash is the beginning of a crusade for one passenger determined to make air travel safer.

programme.title: Mayday Air Disaster
programme.desc: Two 737 planes crash within several years of each other. The similarities are striking but investigators are stumped. It takes a third near miss, for investigators to get the break they need.

programme.title: Mayday Air Disaster
programme.desc: February 19, 1985, a series of small mistakes causes a 747 passenger jet to fall 30,000 feet in just two minutes. Could a lack of sleep be behind the near disaster of China Airlines flight 006?

programme.title: Mayday Air Disaster
programme.desc: Two planes collide in mid-air and crash into a Los Angeles suburb. There are more than 80 victims. The accident set in motion many changes, notably the way planes are monitored.

programme.title: Ice Road Truckers
programme.desc: A blast of arctic air reopens the ice roads; Steph Custance falls victim to severe icing and winds up in a ditch; truckers set out to see if the newly reopened ice crossings are really ready for their big rigs.

programme.title: Ice Road Truckers

programme.title: Ice Road Truckers

programme.title: Christmas Wars
programme.desc: A man dressed as Santa watches four women duke it out in a Massachusetts mall; a woman in Canada wrestles with a large hedge in what may be an attempt to get a free Christmas tree; a pregnant waitress in Pennsylvania gets an unexpected surprise.

programme.title: Christmas Wars

programme.title: Christmas Wars

programme.title: Neighborhood Wars

programme.title: Neighborhood Wars
programme.desc: A commuter is given a neighborly greeting he'll never forget as a neighbor's car smashes into his own vehicle, narrowly avoiding a deadly disaster; in Florida, a family discovers a huge alligator taking a dip in their pool.

programme.title: Neighborhood Wars

programme.desc: You think running a marathon is hard? Try babysitting for the Terrible Tibble Twins. Next, Arthur's family is hosting the once-a-year-is-more-than-enough family reunion. But a major thunderstorm turns this into a reunion to remember.

programme.desc: When the gang discovers a list of their class rankings in Principal Haney's office, they are all taken for a surprise. Brain gets hooked on the latest tween reality show and then starts to ignore his friends and his schoolwork.

programme.title: Arthur: What Is That Thing?/Buster's Best Behavior

programme.title: Arthur: Arthur and The Crunch Cereal Contest/D.W. Flips
programme.desc: A year's supply of Crunch Cereal is in store for the person who can write the new Crunch Cereal jingle, and Arthur is sure he can do it. Next, how hard can it be to do a cartwheel on a balance beam? D.W. is about to find out.

programme.title: Arthur: Flippity Francine/Muffy Takes the Wheel

programme.title: Arthur: Francine's Split Decision/Muffy Goes Metropolitan
programme.desc: Lakewood Elementary is sure to win the big bowling tournament on Saturday, but Francine's cousin is having his Bar Mitzvah at the same time! Muffy happily accepts an invitation to spend the weekend in the bustling Crown City.

programme.title: Arthur: Whip. Mix. Blend./Staycation

programme.title: Arthur: Best Wishes/The Tardy Tumbler
